skill.name: Broken Authentication Testing
skill.description: This skill should be used when the user asks to "test for broken authentication vulnerabilities", "assess session management security", "perform credential stuffing tests", "evaluate password policies", "test for session fixation", or "identify authentication bypass flaws". It provides comprehensive techniques for identifying authentication and session management weaknesses in web applications.
skill.summary: Comprehensive penetration testing skill for identifying broken authentication and session management vulnerabilities in web applications.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a legitimate penetration testing and security assessment skill. All 76 static findings are false positives: the detected 'external_commands' and 'network' patterns are documentation code examples showing legitimate security testing techniques (hydra commands, HTTP request examples, JWT attack demonstrations). The skill properly includes legal requirements for authorization and scope limitations. No actual executable malware or exploit code is present.

content.value_statement: Identify broken authentication and session management vulnerabilities in web applications using comprehensive penetration testing techniques.
actual_capabilities: Perform password policy testing to identify weak password requirements
Test for session management vulnerabilities including fixation and token weakness
Execute credential stuffing attacks using known breached credentials
Assess multi-factor authentication implementations for bypass opportunities
Evaluate password reset flows for token security weaknesses
Analyze rate limiting and account lockout mechanisms
limitations: Requires explicit written authorization before testing
Cannot test applications without valid test accounts
Automated testing may be blocked by CAPTCHA or strict rate limits
Some vulnerabilities require victim interaction to confirm

use_cases.title: Security Assessment for Web Applications
use_cases.description: Conduct comprehensive authentication security audits for web applications during development or production

use_cases.title: Pre-Deployment Vulnerability Screening
use_cases.description: Identify authentication weaknesses before releasing applications to production environments
use_cases.target_user: DevSecOps teams and QA security reviewers

use_cases.title: Authentication Security Training
use_cases.description: Learn authentication testing methodologies and vulnerability identification techniques
use_cases.target_user: Security students and junior penetration testers

prompt_templates.title: Basic Authentication Test
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Broken Authentication Testing skill to assess the authentication security of https://example.com. Start by mapping authentication endpoints and identifying the authentication type used.

prompt_templates.title: Session Management Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a session management security test on the target application. Analyze session token entropy, check for session fixation vulnerabilities, and verify session timeout policies.

prompt_templates.title: Credential Stuffing Assessment
prompt_templates.prompt: Conduct a credential stuffing test using the provided test account list. Evaluate the application defenses against automated credential attacks and document any successful account takeovers.

prompt_templates.title: MFA Bypass Evaluation
prompt_templates.prompt: Test the multi-factor authentication implementation for bypass vulnerabilities. Evaluate OTP brute force protection, enrollment security, and backup code handling.

output_examples.input: Test authentication at https://testapp.example.com for broken authentication vulnerabilities
output: - Implement account lockout after 5 failed attempts
- Add Secure and HttpOnly flags to session cookies

output_examples.input: Analyze session token security for entropy and predictability
output: Risk: HIGH - Tokens are predictable and should not be used

output_examples.input: Test password reset functionality for security weaknesses
output: Vulnerability: Token can be reused across accounts

best_practices: Always obtain written authorization before testing any target application
Document all testing activities and findings for compliance and reporting
Use isolated test accounts to avoid impacting production data
anti_patterns: Testing production systems without explicit authorization
Using real breached credential databases during testing
Exploiting vulnerabilities beyond the scope of the assessment

faq.question: What authorization is required to use this skill?
faq.answer: This skill requires explicit written authorization from the target system owner. Testing without authorization is illegal and unethical.

faq.question: Can this skill test any web application?
faq.answer: Only applications where you have test accounts and explicit authorization. The skill includes legal constraints that must be followed.

faq.question: What tools does this skill use?
faq.answer: The skill provides methodology and examples using common tools like Burp Suite, Hydra, and custom Python scripts for testing.

faq.question: Does this skill perform actual attacks?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides guidance and methodology for authorized security testing. It documents testing techniques without executing them.

faq.question: What authentication vulnerabilities can be identified?
faq.answer: The skill covers password policy weaknesses, session management flaws, credential stuffing, MFA bypass, username enumeration, and password reset vulnerabilities.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for beginner security testers?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes comprehensive documentation from basic concepts through advanced techniques with practical examples.
